mechanism_narrative: Rising real retail sales (RSAFS) indicate strong consumer spending, which directly boosts corporate revenues and earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer spending accounts for approximately 70% of US GDP
failure_condition: If weeks with RSAFS month-over-month increase produce negative median S&P 500 20-day return, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) by more than 0.2 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions, reducing consumer income and spending, which leads to negative S&P 500 returns within the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Sahm rule historically identifies recessions via UNRATE increases
failure_condition: If after a trigger event S&P 500 5-day return is positive in more than 40% of cases, hypothesis rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Low federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) combined with rising housing starts (HOUST) jointly signals accommodative monetary conditions and robust residential investment, which together compress discount rates and boost equity valuations over a 60-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Low rates stimulate housing and reduce corporate discount rates
failure_condition: If the interaction term (low FEDFUNDS and high HOUST) yields negative median S&P 500 60-day return, hypothesis rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) exceeds 3% year-over-year, the economy is overheating, prompting the Federal Reserve to tighten monetary policy, which increases discount rates and depresses equity prices over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Historical tightening cycles follow high inflation, leading to equity declines
failure_condition: If during identified high-inflation periods S&P 500 20-day return is positive in more than 50% of occurrences, hypothesis rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in industrial production (INDPRO) indicates real economic weakness and falling corporate output, which reduces profits and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial production is a coincident indicator of GDP growth
failure_condition: If months with declining INDPRO are followed by positive median S&P 500 20-day return, hypothesis rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts (HOUST) fall below 1,000 thousand units (annualized), the housing market weakens significantly, dragging down household wealth and construction employment, which depresses equity prices over a 60-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing downturns typically precede economic recessions
failure_condition: If HOUST <1000 events yield positive S&P 500 60-day returns in more than 40% of cases, hypothesis rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in payroll employment (PAYEMS) and industrial production (INDPRO) indicate synchronized economic expansion, boosting corporate profits and leading to positive S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Both are coincident indicators of the business cycle
failure_condition: If the joint signal (both rising month-over-month) does not produce positive median S&P 500 20-day return, hypothesis rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) rises above 0.25% after a prolonged near-zero period, it marks the onset of a tightening cycle that increases borrowing costs and reduces equity valuations over the following 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:First rate hike after ZIRP historically triggers equity sell-offs
failure_condition: If after the first breach of 0.25% the S&P 500 20-day return is positive in more than 50% of events, hypothesis rejected.
